plus30_literal_gate.role: The caller-built inline string object that later becomes constructor field +0x30 is conditionally synthesized from the single-character literal '-' when the string-like object at client +0x19C80 is non-empty.

evidence.observation: loads R8 = [rsi +0x19C80], establishing the object inspected before the inline local [rbp -0x70] is built.

decodes the short/heap string layout rooted at [rsi +0x19C80] and skips the builder path when the computed length is zero, making the later literal materialization conditional on a non-empty source object.
loads RDX = 0x140B5E03C immediately before the temporary string-builder call.
headless C-string read resolves the pointed data to the single-character literal '-'.
passes RCX = [rsp +0x70] and RDX = 0x140B5E03C into helper 0x14002A260, proving the literal is first wrapped in a temporary stack-local string object.
normalizes the temporary helper result into begin/end form before the final inline copy.
passes RCX = [rbp -0x70] into helper 0x140031970, which performs the final inline copy used later as wrapper R8.
calls 0x14001B9F0 on [rsp +0x70], showing the literal-backed temporary is torn down after the inline local has been populated.
later passes LEA R8,[rbp -0x70] into wrapper 0x14002EC80.
constructor 0x14058EE40 copies wrapper R8 into persistent field +0x30 via 0x140032150, preserving this caller-built literal object on the retained JS5/bootstrap structure.

careful_inference.statement: On this branch, constructor field +0x30 is not a general endpoint or opaque context string; it is a small marker-like string object built from the literal '-'. The exact semantic meaning of that marker is still unresolved.

supporting_literal_confirmation.observation: headless C-string read reconfirms the formatter literal '%s:%u', which matches the already-mapped host/port formatting path and keeps the new '-' literal separate from the endpoint text fields.

reconciled_conclusions: The previously unresolved source slice copied into [rbp -0x70] now has a concrete branch-local identity: it is a literal '-' marker when client +0x19C80 is non-empty.
This materially narrows constructor field +0x30: at least on this live caller path it is not another formatter-backed endpoint string.

The caller still gates the literal build on a separate string-like object at client +0x19C80, so the remaining unresolved task is to name what that object semantically represents rather than to guess the copied bytes.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: Refines win64c-js5-inline-r8-source-and-post-constructor-string-payload by replacing the vague 'selected source slice' wording with a concrete literal-backed branch for the [rbp -0x70] inline local.

Supports win64c-js5-wrapper-argument-normalization-and-constructor-field-map by further separating constructor field +0x30 from the two formatter-backed text fields and from the independent config/global qword at +0x48.
next_targets: Name the semantic role of the gating object at client +0x19C80 and determine whether other branches build different +0x30 marker strings.
Follow later consumers of constructor field +0x30 to decide whether the '-' marker acts as a sentinel, separator, mode tag, or placeholder.
Keep tracing the retained [rsi +0x18]/[rsi +0x20] payload so the relationship between the +0x30 marker and the later three-string bootstrap descriptor becomes explicit.
